page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor, operating at 2.8 GHz. Part of the m8gn family, this instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached EBS storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gn.8xlarge demonstrates top-tier memory performance, achieving a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths of 412.91 GB/sec and 198.33 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance is also highly competitive, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21484.0 placing it in the top 10%. Database operations are a notable strength, with Passmark database tests scoring in the top tier and Redis SET operations reaching 66.38 million operations per second. Single-core CPU performance and standard integer operations generally fall within the average range.

Given its high memory bandwidth, strong multi-core capabilities, and 100 Gbps network baseline, this server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving. It is also well-suited for software compilation and multi-threaded decompression tasks. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators makes it less suitable for heavy local disk I/O or massive parallel graphics processing, and it exhibits weaker performance in large-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 412.91 GB/sec, and excellent multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21484.0. It excels in database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, though it lacks local storage and hardware acceleration for GPU-dependent workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server with 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring high-speed memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and high-throughput network performance for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory with top-tier uncached read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network traffic
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-core benchmarks
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Best suited for database deployments, software compilation, and static web serving
